posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
This is actually a really fun fight in a lot of ways but mostly because we got two powerhouses with remarkably bad chins so you can almost guarantee a knockout. To put it simply I think Reyes is the better fighter but Johnny is more powerful. I can see either guy winning and in a weird way I kind of want Johnny to win but Im gonna lean the more skilled fighter here.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
This is a horrible matchup for Loopy. She's wicked wasy to take down and she's going against the best wrestler in the division. Pretty obvious how this one is going to go.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, TKO, R2
Here we have the guy with one of the most impressive physiques in the UFC vs a guy who looks like a tub of lard. But god damn is this tub of lard actually really good. I think Azafat probalby deserves someone top 5 but in the meantime I think he has kind of a star making performance here. We havent seen Costa put down with anything since the Izzy fight back in like 2020 but I think he gets slimed out here. Murzakanov has some wicked hand speed and even though Paolo has lighter man skill I think Murz is honestly just a fat middleweight so it kind of cancels out. Only way I see Paulo winning is by surviving and landing those big body kicks in order to slow down Murz as the fight moves into later rounds. More than likely though I think we get another early KO from "the professional"

posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

@D1D4K10 a huge percentage of the stats are from pretty complicated Microsoft Excel statistical models I created. I am basically copying data from UFCstats and manually inputting a few other key stats that I get from elsewhere like ESPN.

Another key place is data scientist, Nate Latshaw's website. I am not sure if I can post links, but just google his name and you should be able to find it. It is called UFC Fight Night Statistical Companion. Nate has a Masters Degree in data science and his website is completely free. I highly suggest following his Twitter, X account as well.

You can go to the "Tale of the Tape" section and see an enormous number of stats that you can't see very many other places. For example you can use his model to see head strike accuracy and defense, takedowns attempted per 5 minutes at distance, strike differential per minute at distance, knockdown/knocked down percentage, how many takedowns landed/conceded a fighter has, and pretty much anything else you could possible want to see.

I also use his data on head strikes, body strikes, leg kicks landed/absorbed to build my projections.

I have been saving all of the tales of the tape and various other data from Nate's model for over a year so I can use it later and make projections. His model will only have the 2 upcoming events.

Anyway, check out Nate's site and I can answer any other questions you guys have about my Excel model. It took me over a year to perfect, I think it has gotten to a good spot. I can make projections for any fight in a few minutes by mostly copy and pasting data from UFCstats.

posts.author: zeddhero
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Van vs Taira
Joshua Van, TKO, R4
Is everyone seriously forgetting the Royval fight? Yes, him and Van are not he same, Royval is more rangy, works in kicks more and uses his reach, but Taira's on-the-feet striking is nothing special and Royval was terrorizing him in that department. I think Van's TDD is good enough to keep this standing for the most part, where he'll just start chipping away at Taira with the volume and eventually finish him.

Chimaev vs Procházka
Jiří Procházka, KO, R5
Picking against Khamzat in 2026 is certainly bold, but I'm not even going to lie, I think Jiri might be the worst overall matchup for him out of perhaps anyone in the UFC. Who are the guys we've seen give Khamzat problems? Not these freaks of nature like Whittaker & DDP who rely almost solely on athleticism, but rather these more technical and careful fighters w a gas tank like Burns & Usman. I think Jiri might get wrestle-f*cked early for sure, but not only is he a better all-around wrestler than DDP, but he's going to be more than savvy enough to keep himself alive and avoid the submissions. Unlike DDP though, Jiri has a gas tank from hell and is most definitely going to turn it on in the later rounds like always, I can see things gettting VERY scary for Khamzat if he starts to gas like the Usman fight and gives more opportunites to Jiri to land on the feet. Guys we have to remember that Khamzat was literally dropped by Durinho Burns, if Jiri can get things going like he did against Khalil in R3 or Rakic in R2 of those fights, I legit think a knockout is in play here for Jiri. Things can change for sure, but I'm most definitely leaning this way right now.

* Edited at 02.21.2026,  1:01 PM ET *

Can anyone genuinely give me an explanation as to why Aaron Pico isn't a top 5 Fighter at 145 right now? I actually have never understood any of his losses. I can excuse his losses to Corrales and Borics considering he was only 22 but I feel like he could have beaten Murphy with the correct game plan. I've always thought Pico has had the tools to become a champion but I could never put my finger on why he's been so unsuccessful? can anyone please help me come to a conclusion?

posts.author: tramenzo
posts.content:
@Haskell

Pico is a combination of defensively reckless and not durable. Having even one of those traits is generally a deal breaker at the highest level of the sport in the smaller weight classes but you absolutely cannot be deficient defensively if you also cannot take a shot. Pico was able to get by in Bellator because all of his opponents, other than Borics, were totally outmatched athletically so he basically could just overpower everyone but once you start fighting physical equals that doesn’t work as you saw against Murphy.

There are defensive adjustments he can make to give himself a better chance against elite opposition in the future, its not a Patchy Mix situation, but if he doesn't make those adjustments and treats the Murphy KO as a fluke occurrence he will just get knocked out again.

You cannot make it in the UFC not respecting the offense coming back at you.

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R4
Jiri wins via pressure, pace, and cardio. He will land a bomb at some point, Ulberg will look to evade, but Jiri does not let you off the hook. He will sprint to follow you and stay in your face.

Van vs. Taira
Joshua Van, DEC
Van keeps it on the feet enough, lands combination after combination at will, because he is a significantly better striker, he is light years faster, he has way better cardio. And Van will be ripping the body early and often, which will just mess Taira up. Van already fought the Japanese style grappler in Rei Tsuruya and stuffed everything.

Reyes vs. Walker
Johnny Walker, TKO, R1
0 chin vs 0 chin. But Reyes got exposed in his last fight IMO. He showed a complete inability to fight while moving forward, I think Reyes will try and be aggressive at some point in the first round and get bombed put cold.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Paulo Costa, DEC
Call me crazy. Costa is unfinishable. The height gap this fight will be stupid. And unlike Rakic Costa will actually use his range. I think Azamat will be lunging in and hitting nothing. I think Costa just picks him apart, eats whatever is coming back at him.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, DEC
Too much too soon for Hokit. Blaydes is still elite.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Suarez boring decision DC glazes her on commentary.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, DEC
BANGER. I like Cub here. Cub protects himself well, has seen it all out there. I think this will be an absolute scrap though.

Holland vs. Brown
Randy Brown, DEC
Brown will basically be landing at will against Holland from an insane range. The question is whether Holland is gonna sneak in a shot that drops or hurts Brown, and then takes it to the ground. Because if Holland hurts Brown, or gets him down I think he can finish him. But I’m inclined to say Holland just emotes and protects himself and loses a decision.

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R1
This is hilarious matchmaking but also really a good fight. I think Pico wins through his youth. He was actually looking damn good imo against Murphy. He just needs to work on not being so open to getting hit, but even then Pitbull doesn’t have one shot knockout power at this stage of his career. I think Pico goes out and bullies him because he doesn’t have any fear of what’s coming back at him.

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
Gamrot gets him. Decision. Basically exactly what he did to Ludovit Klein. The gate will be kept. I honestly think that using volume striking to try and stop grapplers is not the move. Ribovics tries to land with volume, throws 250 strikes a fight, does not get tired. Gamrot also doesn’t get tired, and will be able to keep Ribovics down on the ground imo.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, DEC
Washed vs mega washed. But you get to pick which one is which. Flip a coin, that’s the winner.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, TKO, R2
Padilla looked damn good against Bonfim. If yall want to watch someone actually quit in the cage watch Padilla vs Bonfim. Padilla wins this with his superior durability and cardio.

posts.content:

@madaMMA  Couple issues with that:

Aaron is a glass cannon, all gas, no breaks, and has a chin spottier than a Dalmatian. Patricio is not just a counter striker, he’s a bladed stance counter striker, which are extremely rare and Patricio, even in the twilight of his career, still has speed and power in his hands, as well as knowing how to use zig zag footwork to bait boxing exchanges to land his rear hand. If Aaron plans on using his typical gameplan of marching forward and scrap until he sees an opportunity to go for a bodylock or single leg, he’s inevitably going to get clipped, unless he chooses to get Patricio’s back early and try to choke him, which is unlikely since Patricio has very solid grappling defense, and Aaron hasn’t had a submission victory since 2021. And that was against Aiden Lee. Who sucks.

I understand Patricio has looked very unremarkable as of lately in comparison to his prime years, but stylistically Aaron winning this looks very slim IMO.

posts.author: lettmma
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Van vs. Taira
Tatsuro Taira, SUB, R2
Taira Submission. Van's run at 125 has been crazy. His fight with Royval was a breakout performance and one of the best scraps I have seen. But I think Taira has the facilities to stop Van here. Watching the Tsuruya fight back, he struggled to manipulate Van's balance whenever they would clinch. Tsuruya did not have the facilities to take Van's back, off balance, and secure a dominant position. Taira has this ability in his game. Taira dominated Royval and Moreno in the grappling. Taira did leave his head on the centerline in the Royval fight, but head movement is a hole Taira can add and learn quickly. Van will have to develop a complete cage wrestling system. He will have to account for back take defense against a body lock, submission defense, avoiding being flatten out and trips against the cage. If Taira gets his back, I think the fight will be over shortly.

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R3
Jiri KO. Jiri's fights are the most fascinating thing to watch. Carlos is a really great technical striker. But I think Jiri will implement his game no matter what. I do not think Ulberg will be able to keep his wits in the chaos. Jiri has a controlled chaos/recklessness. He isn't going to be reckless like Meinfeild because Jiri has great finishing instincts and knows how to time his finishing sequences. Jiri will get hurt this fight but he is more durable than Reyes, in hindsight people knew Reyes run wouldn't last. Jiri isn't going to be scared to enter the pocket and take one to give one. His head movement has improved and he rolled well with punches from Rountree and Hill. Once Jiri lands a good shot on in Round 2 or 3 I think we will see a huge momentum shift and a spectacularly dramatic finish by Jiri.

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R3
Jiri's got great cardio, a good chin, and good power with a variety of strikes. Ulberg is on a pretty good run, but c'mon look at the guys he beat: Blanchowitz coming off injury after a long layoff where he barely beat him, squeked out a decision win against Oezdemir, and Reyes who has no chin at this point. Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, TKO, R2
The line on this fight baffles me a bit. I know Costa looked decent in his last fight and brings lighter man skill to 205, but he's never fought at 205. He does have a chance to outpoint Murzakanov to a decision, but I just don't see it happening. I'll take the guy who's undefeated at rank 6 who has fought at this weight class vs. the guy who is barely ranked at 185 whose only wins in the last 6 years was Roman Kopylov and 40 year old Rockhold

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
About as 50/50 as it gets. I think Reyes is more technical? Both their chins are shot. Walker hasn't KOed someone in awhile though and isn't as wild and powerful as he was when he got into the UFC. His KO against Zhang wasn't really a KO since Zhang's leg gave out and the fight was over. I think Walker won't be throwing with enough power to put out Reyes and Dom will find the chin

Radtke vs. Prado
Charles Radtke, DEC

posts.author: _Vis_
posts.content:
Ulberg wins this not only for his power but his accuracy and timing, which are equally if not more important than power for finding knockouts imo. Yeah it's possible Jiri can just rush him and find a finish, but we've seen Jiri hurt to varying degrees by other guys besides Pereira and we've seen him slow down his process somewhat in an attempt to be technical or minimize damage. Carlos can fight going forwards and backwards and the last time he was rushed by an athletic fighter with ridiculous power (Menifield) he scored a clean KO.

You CAN hurt Jiri if you're not Pereira. You CAN confuse him/stay safe with good footwork and range management. This will probably another case of Jiri's lack of defense biting him in the ass. Again, Ulberg is too fast, powerful and accurate for his punches to have no effect, even if he doesn't KO Jiri

posts.author: _Vis_
posts.content:
@Fast_Tulip Reyes made Jiri chicken dance and back up before the upkick with a well timed left, and I may be misembering but I think Jiri was hurt several times over the course of his battle with Glover. Also, regardless of Menifield's standing in the division I think it's fair to say he's one of the most explosive guys in LHW, more so than Jiri even imo, and despite that Ulberg was able to keep calm in the middle of a blitz and counter perfectly

When it comes to evasive footwork, yeah I do think Ulberg's is better than Pereira mostly backing up in straight lines when he fought Jiri.

Lastly Jiri doesn't always charge in. He took a lot more of a cautious approach in his fights against Pereira and Khalil, likely out of fear of being KO'd, so I wouldn't be surprised to see him fight the same here. If he does, then he def won't out-technique Carlos

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Gamrot vs Ribovics
Gamrot DEC

Really fun fight but this one doesn’t make a ton of sense. I know Gamrot is the guy they throw to prospects they like now, but Ribovics is not top 15 level to me; actually, I don’t even think he’s top 25 level. Don’t get me wrong, Ribovics is one of the most entertaining guys in the division but the excitement doesn’t translate to skill level at all. He goes life and death with everyone, regardless of skill level. Again, he’s had some epic wars but I really don’t think Ribovics is some special talent. He was one scorecard away from losing to Zellhuber, got absolutely outclassed on the mat against Loik, lost decisively to Haqparast (if you think that was a robbery, I have no comment), etc. He puts up a dogfight against anyone and is very hard to fight because he doesn’t **** off, but he’s also extremely flawed. His tdd is good on paper but that’s because he’s fought guys who death gas and panic shoot on him; his tdd is fine for what it is but it’s nothing special at all, the stat is inflated because he faced something like 40 td attempts in his first couple of fights. He has decent scrambling and a great will to get to his feet, but his wrestling/grappling is really bad. Anyone who tries hard enough to take him down can do so, and sometimes he shows no resistance at all. Gamrot is not known for his control and does appear to be declining ever so slightly, but I don’t see how he can’t score free tds here. Ribovics can maybe defend the first and second, but the consecutive tds are going to be very hard to defend. Gamrot is a high level scrambler and chain wrestler, and even in current form I think he is levels above Ribovics in that category. I do think Ribovicds can get to his feet but seeing guys like Kirk and Loik easily take him down is super concerning. IMO Klein was a harder fight because he actually has good tdd, and Gamrot showed levels there. I do think Ribovics will get to his feet but I really don’t see how his wrestling/grappling is on par here. Gamrot is not going to slow down and has the cardio to match Ribovics, so without that crutch I think he’s at a big disadvantage. Ribovics is the better striker but even that I think is massively overrated. Ribovics is a complete zombie and his pressure is awesome to watch, but that too is overrated because of how exciting he is. He’s no doubt a better striker here but I don’t think Gamrot is ****** every second this is on the feet. Not that Gamrot is going to win the striking, but Ribovics is plodding and doesn’t move his head, so he can land here. Ribovics also has insanely overrated power and is a punching bag himself. Again, it won’t matter because Gamrot isn’t a striker, but for someone who’s supposed to be this elite brawler, Ribovics is a punching bag. Haqparast soundly outboxed him and the Zellhuber fight was extremely close (aging like milk as well). Gamrot is chinny but I think Ribovics’s power is insanely overrated. He has one ko in the ufc and it was a head kick against McKinney; he lacks power and really isn’t the ko threat he seems. He can def drop Gamrot but I don’t think Gamrot is in the usual amount of danger on the feet. I think this is goong to be a repeat of the RDA fight/Loik fight; Ribovics is probably going to rock him at some point but I don’t think his wrestling is on par, and he will get smothered.

Suarez vs Godinez
Godinez DEC

The odds and pick % are really confusing to me here. I’m a big Loopy hater and always have been, but I think this is a great matchup for her. I think Loopy is at her peak, we’ve seen she hasn’t been able to fix the holes in her game and has continued to struggle with the same styles over and over again. Normally this would be a good reason for me to fade (as I have in her last two), except for the fact that Suarez is super washed. She’s still a very good wrestler but the injuries have taken their toll. Since coming back in 2023, she has not looked the same whatsoever. Her physicality seems to have declined a lot, her wrestling not nearly as effective, she pulls guillotine on every td attempt now (horrible iq), and her cardio/striking has regressed. The latter two were bad in her prime, but they’ve gotten much worse in older age. Losing to Weili is nothing to scoff at but Suarez got completely dismantled there. She then came back against Lemos and looked completely regressed. Even the rounds she won her control looked mid and it was very difficult for her to get the takedown; she easily controlled her on top but it took her forever to get to the mat. She also death gassed bad in r3 and nearly got finished as well. I’ve always really disliked Loopy because she never gameplans and never uses her very overrated wrestling. I think her offensive wrestling is extremely overrated and honestly not good, but defensively she’s fine. She can be taken down but it’s difficult to hold her down and her get ups are pretty good. Loopy has horrendous fight IQ but I don’t think Suarez is as good of a grappler as the girls that have given her trouble in the past (elite wrestler, mid grappler). Suarez also doesn’t wrestle like a conventional fighter, her entire game is based on ankle picks/low singles. She shoots very low and isn’t afraid to ankle bite, so she can then transition to the body lock or get a td from there. It works on girls who aren’t prepared but against a fellow wrestler, I don’t think she can get away with a lot of her usual stuff. Suarez’s entries have looked slow and telegraphed recently as well, so I think this will make things even more difficult. Suarez is also declining a lot physically/athletically, where athleticism is like the only thing keeping Loopy ranked, as well as being in her physical prime. Suarez looks extremely weak physically to me, and her strength has not looked the same; I think she is going to find it very difficult to get/hold Loopy down. She’s also had so many injures as well and those are clearly catching up with her. I normally like fading Loopy because she is far too content to strike and is a bad striker, but in this matchup she has all the advantage. Suarez is a hilariously bad striker and does not like extended exchanges. She has no defense, poor body language, and bad reactions to being hit. Loopy should have the advantage on the feet here by a large margin. She normally doesn’t separate well and has no power but Suarez does not have anything to offer on the feet. Loopy has slowed down pretty bad in her last two fights, but imo those were really bad matchups for her; as she was facing two brawlers who would pressure her. In this fight, Loopy can do all of the pressuring and is facing someone with much worse cardio. Suarez slows down bad after r2 and even quicker when facing resistance; against Weili for example she had 4 minutes of cardio and was done the second she showed resistance to the td. Loopy has better tdd than both Weili/Lemos, so I think her showing resistance will make Suarez slow down a lot quicker. Loopy has a terrible resume and this would easily be her best win, but Suarez is not at all who she used to be. She is one dimensional and has declined a lot. Loopy has a lot of flaws but I think she has the tdd to keep this standing, and her normal flaws of bad striking/cardio won’t matter here since Suarez is worse in both categories, as well as stopping attempts to win and going into survival mode once she slows down.

Mederos vs Padilla
Padilla SUB RD 2

I like this one a lot more than the original booking. Both guys are undefeated in the ufc and the winner probably gets a big fight next. Padilla came in as a regional guy with a ****** record but has actually developed into a very well rounded and skilled fighter. Mederos looks the part and has good athleticism, but his record is a facade. I won’t be too rude since I know Mederos will read this and he genuinely seems like a nice person, but he just doesn’t do it for me. He’s no longer a FacX guy but you can see the influence in his style; 80% of his game is footwork and feints. He looks the part of a good striker but everything about his game is lacking substance. He does a lot of good work with his lower body but his boxing is lacking. He mostly just spams calf kicks at range and feints a lot. He doesn’t have a ton of power and his boxing is kind of mediocre as well. He has good punching mechanics but again, he’s lacking substance; it feels like he just throws stuff to throw it and he doesn’t have a great process. Padilla’s striking is the exact opposite; he’s not flashy at all but has a very high level process. I was not a big fan of him when he first came to the ufc but watching him back, he’s a very good striker. His muay thai fundamentals are so sharp and he always has a great vision/very high guard. He picks his shots well, never panics, always stays safe, and has great shot selection. I’m still very salty he beat my boy Rongzhu but I was very impressed with the mid-fight adjustments he made there. He was getting pieced up early but adjusted his footwork, got Rongzhu to chase, and then broke his orbital with a counter elbow. His elbows in particular are pretty nasty, he winds them up big and picks them well. I think the best thing about Padilla is all his experience; he paid his dues on the regional scene and has leveraged that experience in the ufc. He gameplans extremely well and is very good at adjusting on the fly. I see Mederos having a very difficult time winning the striking here. He is a better athlete than Padilla, but Padilla knows how to check kicks (Mederos’s main weapon), is a much better boxer with a huge reach advantage here, stays much more defensively sound (He keeps a high guard at all times while Mederos’s hands are low and his chin up at all times), and just has a better process overall. I think he can fight Mederos at range but also box him up in close if he chooses to do so. Mederos has not fought many capable strikers and the one he did (Quinones) arguably beat him imo; even if you disagree, he caused a lot of issues with his boxing. Padilla not only has superior boxing but he has the skillset to deal with the leg kicks as well. Mederos is hittable, with little power and a poor process so I see him having a ton of issues on the feet here. Padilla is also going to grapple here, and he should rinse Mederos in this area. Mederos’s grappling is very bad to put it lightly. He does have a wrestling background and I believe he has some sort of colored belt in BJJ so he is serviceable, but his tdd and fight iq on the mat is godawful. He was seconds to losing to Hubbard via grappling and despite a dominant win over Choinski last fight, Mederos still got his back taken more times than I liked to see. Padilla isn’t some elite grappler but he can wrestle, is a black belt, and knows what he’s doing there. I don’t think he’s going to come in wrestling heavy but I do expect him to mix it in. I think he can get Mederos down and should be a step ahead there. Mederos has been able to survive bad spots but if you watch his last two it’s clear his grappling is not ufc level. He makes awful mistakes on the mat and constantly finds himdself in bad spots. I think someone is going to expose him for this badly, and it could happen here. Padilla has a good back take and decent top control; as well as easily being the best guy Mederos has fought thus far. I actually think Padilla can sub him; he is a deceptive finisher and I don’t think he will just cruise on the back unlike prior opponents of Mederos. These two are only a year apart yet Padilla has more wins than Mederos has fights, and has been competing in MMA for much longer. This experience goes a long way and Padilla has been able to leverage it in every fight thus far. Again, I don’t want to be too harsh because Mederos is talented, but I do not think his skills are up to par with the ufc level. He has had cupcake matchmaking and looked very pedestrian against everyone. He has also pulled out of every fight he’s had booked against real comp but is then magically healthy a few weeks later to take on a short notice jobber; this is very suspect and comes across as ducking to me. Padilla is unassuming but he’s actually very talented and has a deep bag of skills. I think he wins this one fairly easily and I think he can even fund a sub here. Mederos’s grappling is very bad and someone is going to expose him for it soon, if not here.

Luque vs Gastelum
Gastelum KO RD 1

Really good matchmaking in terms of career trajectory but much like with Prado in the first fight, Luque fights are a very difficult watch. Luque should’ve retired after the Neal fight but I think he’s still here because fighting is all he knows. Watching his performances post-Neal Luque looks disinterested, terrified of getting hit, and quits when the going gets tough. He did show a flash of his old self against Themba but other than that he is a complete shell of what he was. Gastelum is pretty washed up himself but he’s still got his signature chin. He doesn’t really give a **** about fighting anymore but his wins have been pretty decisive and he doesn’t have any “bad” losses. KG has looked like **** in some of his losses but they’ve all been to top contenders at 170/85. Luque isn’t losing to bad guys either for the most part (besides Holland), but he looks abysmal. With his chin and confidence gone, the really bad flaws in Luque’s game are wide open: he is slow, plodding, and a stationary target. His last two fights were somehow worse than I remembered; Holland rocked him in nearly every single exchange before he did his classic quitjob takedown, and Joel finished him twice in the first two rounds. He ko’ed Luque with a punch to the eye but they called it a foul, then at the end of r2 Luque needed assistance to get to his corner, and you could audibly hear him say “I can’t fight anymore” but his corner forced him out there. He then spent all of r3 in a defensive position on the ground and Joel showed him mercy. Luque fighting in the cage is genuinely a danger to his health, and he doesn’t even enjoy being in there because he finds the door the second things go south. Gastelum is on his way out too but he’s mentally checked out, Luque does not have what it takes to compete anymore. Luque is also moving to 185 which makes no sense, because he’s going to face harder hitters and the weight cut won’t help his damaged brain. It also benefits KG a ton, as he gets to face another WW without cutting down. KG is very low volume and will box with Luque but I feel like he has faster hands. KG is soooo lazy and hasn’t finished someone in close to a decade, but I think he can find the chin here. He still has power as we saw in his last fight and even landed a couple shots that stunned Pyfer badly. Luque isn’t a complete glass jaw but he gets hurt with every combination that lands and again, I really don’t know if he can take clean strikes anymore. He was reacting so badly to everything that Holland/Alvarez landed on him. He also looks for a way out when hurt so all KG needs to do is land one flush overhand and Luque will curl up and quit. KG is a fat 170 but he’s still got the 185 power, I really don’t think Luque should be moving up to face harder hitters. I know KO is a risky pick and this likely becomes a really boring decision where KG hurts him and lays on top for 8+ minutes, but I think the first strike he lands flush will make Luque quit. He did give up to a legal shot against Joel, and what I found even more concerning was literally 2 minutes into the fight his face was already demolished. Not only is his durability gone but he doesn’t even wear damage well anymore either. Both are showing up for paychecks but KG makes a more competitive effort to win fights. I am of the opinion that Luque should not be sanctioned anymore, I really hope he retires after this; especially if he does end up getting ko’ed again.

Radtke vs Prado
Radtke KO RD 2

This fight doesn’t make a ton of sense given their positioning in the divisions, but this is going to be a banger to kick things off. Both these guys are sluggers who are not afraid to take one to give one. I’m normally a big Radtke hater and never like to back him, but this is a very easy fight for him imo. Prado looks good on paper but he is a danger to himself; he should not be in the ufc and is one of the only fighters I genuinely feel bad watching. The reason he’s a hard watch is because Prado takes ungodly amounts of damage in every fight and lacks the skills to stop any of it. Taking one to give one can be effective in the UFC, but Prado lacks the technical ability to stop taking damage and offer something back. His last three fights he has absorbed a whopping 244 strikes, with the majority being to the head. Radtke is someone I usually like to fade but I don’t think Prado can do much of anything to him. He is going to fight the fight Radtke wants and lacks the danger factor to do anything about it. I usually don’t like Radtke because he’s a very limited striker, but Prado is even more so. Both guys are low level sluggers but Radtke possesses some actual striking ability. He is primarily a boxer who lives and dies by the left hook, but he does have good power and pressure. Even against Malott he was landing good and making him uncomfortable despite getting KTFO. Radtke is pretty chinny but I don’t think that will matter since Prado is not a good finisher. He has a 100% finish rate but it’s all against tomato cans; he has not looked very powerful or even threatening against any real comp. Prado’s head does not move off of the centerline and his defense is just his chin. It’s a little difficult to watch Prado imo, because he’s so young yet takes life-altering damage in every fight. The most alarming thing to me is that he’s getting outclassed by guys who aren’t even that good. Zellhuber is a flop yet beat Prado within an inch of his life, Matthews only looks good against mid and put a boxing masterclass on Prado, and the Veretennikov fight…. No comment; just watch that one for yourself, some of the worst display of fight iq ever. Prado is very durable but I think he can get ko’ed here. He does not defend any strikes coming his way and was very close to getting ko’ed last fight. Veretennikov flash ko’ed him with that slam and Prado was hurt for the rest of the fight; I think if Veretennikov had any fight iq he would’ve finished that fight. Radtke doesn’t have insane power but I really don’t see Prado being able to take more punishment here. He’s also not a real 170 at all and doesn’t have the skills to close the size gap in any of his fights. Prado may try to go for takedowns but his grappling is really bad. He can’t wrestle and has a bad bottom game as well. Radtke is a black belt (I believe, at least a brown belt) and can grapple himself; it’s nothing special but it’s at least serviceable. This is going to be a banger but I think Radtke ****s Prado up here. Prado being in the UFC is a danger to his health, he doesn’t have the skills to hang here and takes way too much damage. I think Radtke will land that left hook repeatedly and put Prado out. He’s very durable but you can’t continue to take the punishment he has fight to fight.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Swanson vs Landwehr
Swanson KO RD 3

This is such a great sendoff fight for Cub. I think he should’ve walked away after the Billy Q fight but this one has potential to be just as insane as last time. Cub is an old school brawler and Nate is a guy who takes a beating early and breaks his opponents late. I get why Cub is the dog here, and watching his last two back he definitely is more washed than he seems, but I also think this is a good matchup for him. Nate is 4.5 years younger than him but still pretty old himself, as well as being on a bad downturn. Nate is an awesome fighter to watch but I’ve never liked backing him because I think he’s overachieved in the UFC. He used to be such a dawg but he would always take an ass whooping in most fights before coming back. Imo he’s never been overly skilled, just a crazy tough brawler with heart. He’s looked completely awful in his last two fights as well, getting finished in both and viciously outclassed in the former. He did win a round off of Morgan, but right as it seemed he was shifting the momentum he got insta-ko’ed in r3. Cub has been having a lot of success in the latter half of his career but he definitely is slowing down. His style is very unique but also outdated; he struggles a lot with leg kicks and gets hit a lot with his hands down style. I don’t like backing guys who are open about retiring but in Cub’s case I don’t think he is going to perform any worse than normal. I don’t like his age and layoff either but against an equally washed vet I don’t think it’s going to be a huge detriment. Cub has declined a lot physically but the skills are still there, Nate on the other hand is pretty washed imo. He looks insanely slow and can’t take damage anymore either; his lone win in the last 3 years was another comeback as well. Emmers had him on skates early. I don’t think he was ever the most skilled guy to begin with, and without his chin he’s an above average striker with no defense. Cub’s last fight was an ugly back and forth war but in the context of this matchup I think it’s a really good look. The reason being, Nate and Billy fight very similarly in terms of approach. They take a beating early and get better as the fight goes on, using their cardio as a weapon. Cub not only survived the Billy Q swamp, he was actually able to stop it from taking off; every time it looked like the momentum was shifting, Cub would gain some footing with big shots. Billy is shot to pieces but I think his pace is a much bigger issue than Nate’s; Nate doesn’t throw nearly as much. Cub is declining physically but I don’t think his chin is an issue and his cardio definitely isn’t. This is a problem imo because Nate heavily relies on his opponents having these holes; if Cub isn’t going to slow to his pace and is willing to fight in the pocket, I think he will find plenty of opportunities to land. Cub has been ko’ed twice in recent years but they were a liver kick and a leg kick (at BW, where he never should’ve been). Nate hits hard but I don’t think he will chin Cub; Cub does get hit a lot but he doesn’t have bad reactions and knows how to roll with a strike. I’m actually more worried about Cub getting caught in a clinch exchange and Nate doing some dumb **** like rolling for an anaconda lol. I actually think Cub is much more durable than Nate; Nate gets hurt in every single fight and has really bad defense. Cub is 42 so his body may quit on him, but he still seems to be operating fine to me. Nate on the other hand has been on the decline for a while and taken a lot of damage these last few fights. I think this is a favorable style matchup for him, and you know Cub won’t gas/can take a shot, which are the two things you have to worry about when fighting Nate.

Pitbull vs Pico
Pico KO RD 1

Bellator never die; these two were on a collision course prior to the PFL merger but it never got finalized. I’m really glad the UFC booked this one, not only is it a good fight that should’ve happened years ago, but I also think it’s a good bounceback for Pico. His debut went as badly as possible but I still believe in his talent. Is he ever going to be a champ/title challenger? No, but I do think he is a solid top 5-10 guy with a well rounded skillset. I sadly don’t think he’s ever going to fix his bad habits, and the damage on his chin has already been done. However, the majority of his losses are due to really bad career management. He has taken matchups that normal prospects should never be taking, and once he finally gets some momentum things go wrong. The Kennedy fight was freak bad luck but the fact he dislocated his shoulder mid fight speaks to his poor durability; the man is a freak of all freak athletes and has an elite skillset but he has the durability of a glass house and is too aggressive for his own good. This is why he got smoked by Lerone as well. That loss was really bad (in terms of damage taken) but again I think that was just bad career management; I picked him to win but that was not an easy debut whatsoever. If this was the Pitbull from 2-3 years ago I think he’d cause a lot of problems but now, not so much. Pitbull is still really skilled but I think the low volume, counter striking heavy style he’s adopted in the latter half of his career is really going to **** him over against the ultra aggressive Pico. Of course the counter to this is that Pico will run into a shot and get countered (sadly a very real possibility), but imo the way he fights won’t give many openings for Pibull. Pitbull is a very skilled striker but the problem is in current form he is way too slow and not durable enough to deal with this kind of pressure. The chaos Pico creates is also something he will struggle with, Pitbull needs a low volume sparring match at range to work. Ever since the Borics fight Pitbull has been really passive and he did a lot of damage to his chin with the Pettis/Suzuki fights b2b. He looked solid against Ige but we now know that Ige is just extremely shot, as well as being the perfect style for Ige on the feet; he stood with Pitbull at range for most of the fight. Pitbull has also been winning with his grappling but I don’t think he will have much success with that here. Pico’s wrestling is very legit so it will be hard to outwrestle him anyways, but the aforementioned pressure is going to keep Pitbull on his backfoot and won’t present opportunities for him to get in on the hips. Pico’s chin is always a concern but on the feet I think he should cook Pitbull. Pico’s boxing is very good and he is going to have a huge speed advantage here; both in terms of movement and hand speed. He’s a super explosive athlete and I don’t think current form Pitbull is equipped to deal with that. His durability is not what it used to be either, he’s been hurt in his last 5 fights and ever since the Suzuki fight he reacts especially poorly to damage. He still has good recovery but his ability to take shots is definitely declining. Pico throws super hard and I think he can overwhelm him with those nasty body shots he rips. Pico has the ability to mix in takedowns but I don’t see him having a ton of success either; Pitbull is a high level grappler and has never really been outwrestled in his career. Pico is very explosive so I can see him scoring takedowns, but I don’t see him winning with control. I believe in Pico as a fighter still and I think current form Pitbull is not a crazy dangerous matchup for him. I think Pitbull is too slow to expose the over aggression of Pico, and I think his chin has been a bad KO waiting to happen again for some time.

Holland vs Brown
Holland SUB RD 2

The Holland gimmick has now fully run its course; this is his first time not on a main card since 2020. I started to stop liking Holland after the Wonderboy fight, his downfall since has been well documented. He was always someone who preferred money over winning, but he barely makes serious attempts anymore and even when he wins it’s usually on thin margins. His last two have been especially bad, losing to people well below his skill level and pulling massive stunts in both. He should not have continued against Malott and the D-rod fight is one of the biggest fumbles I’ve ever seen; he actually almost won that fight in r3 as well, but was too hurt and gassed to capitalize. This fight is hilarious to me though, as I really hate Holland yet kind of think he wins this one. Holland is one of my least favorite fighters currently but so is Brown; he has never been able to break past the top 25 and is insanely overrated in the majority of his fights. He’s always too big of a favorite or lined way too close against good fighters (Bonfim fight for example). I can’t really pick at him for struggling with bad comp since it goes the same way with Holland, but imo there is a difference between the two. Both are really skilled but can’t put it together, the difference is Holland is just a lazy sack of ****, where Brown is genuinely bad (imo). He has a very good ufc record and has produced some cool highlights, but Brown constantly has mental lapses that cost him fights. He has never learned how to check leg kicks, doesn’t use his reach well at all, has a glass chin, a very sus bottom game, and has bad defense too. Neither guy has fixed the holes in their games but Holland has at least consistently been fighting/struggling with guys in the rankings for a while; Brown has been dropping rounds to unranked guys for years. He had a razor thin fight with Turman, got dropped bad against Dalby (somehow people ignored that fact), very nearly lost to Khaos, had a near 50/50 fight with Trinaldo after dropping him r1, etc. He’s had so many fights against undermatched guys where he should style on them, yet ends up making it very competitive with them. Again, I don’t want to harp on him too much because Holland is more flawed, but there is a difference in competition here. I think Brown should be the favorite given how awful Holland has looked, but I weirdly am kind of confident in him here. For one this is going to be a striking match, which is already favorable for Holland. Brown can get takedowns but I don’t see him having a ton of success; he’s never had a wrestling heavy gameplan and I don’t think he can hold Holland down the whole fight. Even if he does, Randy is not the best grappler and doesn’t do a lot on top. I’ve also seen him get his back taken a few times, as well as being just as lost as Holland on his back; who can forget the Price KO loss as well. I don’t think Holland is going to end up on top or anything like that, but I would not be surprised if Randy ends up on bottom or fumbles something from the top; it would not be the first time and I genuinely don’t think he is a good grappler. Holland has no wrestling but his bjj is still good and he is active off of his back. Holland’s striking has looked alarmingly bad as of late; he still has power and his long reach but he throws everything from his hips and just swings with no setup. His chin also seems to be declining a lot which is really bad as that was one of the only things keeping him around. I do think Brown is the better technical striker (looks much better visually as well), but he has some really fatal flaws on the feet that I think are going to **** him over here. Brown has sick movement with his hands down style and crafty footwork, but his defense is really bad. You need to be perfect at all times with this kind of defensive style, as hands down leaves your chin wide open. Brown is shockingly bad at measuring his reach and as a result leaves himself open in the pocket all the time. He has a huge size advantage against everyone yet fights heavily in the pocket. I think this is going to be a huge issue here against Holland, as they have almost identical frames. Holland has a rech advantage and still has power; I actually noticed in the Luque fight he was hurting him with every shot, as well as nearly knocking d-rod out multiple times in r3. His technique is whacky but Holland is still very dangerous; I just can’t unsee Brown getting overzealous in the pocket and Holland landing clean on his chin. He has not fought many people with his frame and gets touched up all the time despite having a big size advantage. This shouldn’t matter for Holland since level of comp means nothing to him (the one benefit of his journeyman mentality lol). Holland is not one to target weaknesses or gameplan, but if he sticks to leg kicks while boxing Brown, he is going to dominate. Brown is very heavy on his lead leg and despite a 10+ year UFC career, he has still not figured out how to check a low kick. Bonfim exposed him very badly for this and it has been an issue since his debut. The last point I want to make is that Brown never wins important fights. This may not seem it, but imo this is a big fight for both; they are fighting to maintain relevancy, I think the loser is going to be out of the UFC in 2-3 years and I think they will stop getting preferential treatment with a loss as well. As bad as Holland is, he has historically done well against unranked comp (I know that’s changed a lot but still) and has won the “important” fights. Brown on the other hand has found a way to lose every crossroads fight in his career. He’s now 35 and hit his ceiling. I despise Holland but I think he’s got enough left in the tank to handle business here.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Prochazka vs Ulberg
Prochazka KO RD 4

This is a fantastic main event. I like both of these guys a lot and both are great strikers so this should be a war. Ulberg has had some stinkers before but Jiri will bring it out of him; it’s not possible for a Jiri fight to be bad. This is a tricky one to call as both of these guys are pretty bad styles for the other imo. Jiri is usually outmatched on a technical level in most fights, but he has an indomitable will and elite athleticism/finishing ability. If you aren’t able to put his lights out, he is going to find a way to finish you. He never fights a pretty fight and forces a war out of everyone. This is a scary fight because Ulberg is not only a better striker than Jiri, he also has the ability to put his lights out. The Khalil fight was a really bad look to me as Jiri was completely lost on his timing and looked really off compared to how he usually fights. He was standing at range way too much and not making it the ugly fight he needed. Once he woke up in r3 he beat the **** out of Khalil and it wasn’t even close, but those first 10 minutes were odd. He cannot start that same way against Ulberg, or else he’s going to fall behind big time. I expect Jiri to lose the first round anyways, but he has to pressure or at least make Ulberg fight at a high tempo. Ulberg is a super high level striker and has always been underrated because of how his career started. He has the leg kicks to stall Jiri’s movement and his straight left is absolute money; he’s one punched multiple people with it during this current run. I am worried Jiri runs into that and his lights get put out. He gets hurt in every fight and has been KO’ed before, Ulberg is definitely one to be able to do that. However, Ulberg needs his fights to go perfectly. Ever since the Kennedy fight, Ulberg has adopted that classic, patient point fighting style we see out of all the top guys at CKB. His striking is amazing (one of the best in the division imo) but if you look at every fight since Kennedy, Ulberg has gotten the exact fight he needs to succeed, as well as nuking the bad style matchups (Alonzo, Nicolae) instantly. I expect Jiri to be a step behind early, but unless Ulberg either compromises his leg/something else badly, or finishes him in 10 minutes, this is going to be an increasingly difficult fight for him. When Jiri gets into his zone he is a menace. Ulberg will still be able to land on him but as we saw in the Kennedy fight, his technique and defense declines heavily when the fight gets ugly. I don’t think he has bad cardio, but I do believe he will slow down in similar fashion once Jiri wakes up; that’s not even a knock on him, as Jiri does this to everyone. Ulberg has not been in an ugly back and forth war like that since it happened, which is why I worry the swamp is going to form very quickly once Jiri finds his groove. This is a pretty binary fight and the odds represent that; I think Ulberg either finishes him early, or Jiri takes over late and gets a classic comeback finish. Ulberg is the much better striker and has the ability to put Jiri out, but he’s also the older and significantly less experienced guy. Not that I expect rookie mistakes or anything from Ulberg, but that stuff does matter in these close and ugly fights. My initial lean was that Ulberg is going to get it done but the more I watched, I kind of think Jiri is going to grit it out. Ulberg is super high level and I usually always back him in these fights, but he hasn’t fought someone who won’t respect him in a while. I thought he won both but he was in some very competitive fights with Jan/Volkan, so while I do think he’s an elite striker, he is not untouchable.

Costa vs Murzakanov
Murzakanov KO RD 2

Very random matchup but I think this should be a really fun fight. Idk why Costa is moving to 205, especially after he gained some momentum in his last fight. This is a closer fight than I first thought once I looked into it, but I still feel like Azamat should win comfortably. I actually feel like there is some recency bias on Costa here, and I’m saying this as someone who picked him confidently over Kopylov. Costa did look good there but I expected him to, as Kopylov is very low level imo (not a top 15 guy at all). The commentators were talking like it was a complete masterclass but Costa didn’t look that spectacular. He won r1-2 very comfortably and hurt Kopylov twice, but Costa also gassed out badly in r3 and even got rocked a few times. I expect his cardio to be better at 205 but he’s never had amazing cardio and he dumped hard in that r3; he actually brought the round back but for the first 3 minutes he was losing pretty clearly and getting put on the backfoot by Kopylov. Kopylov has terrible cardio himself so this was not a good look to me. Again, Costa should hold up better with a smaller weight cut, but I don’t think he holds some massive cardio edge against Azamat. Azamat’s cardio is pretty bad, and he has had some bad looks in the past against Alonzo/Jacoby, but it’s not like Costa is some cardio machine. One problem I do have in this matchup is that Costa does fight through being tired much better than Azamat; Costa gasses out every fight because he fights so stupid and aggressively, forcing you to drown with him, where Azamat goes on the defensive and tries to survive. I admit that this is a bad look, especially against this bruiser style in Costa, but I also don’t think Costa is some super high level striker. I actually think he’s pretty overrated, he holds no good wins (other than ancient Yoel but even then that was lowkey a robbery) and he’s got a very sloppy approach. Costa takes one to give one and loves to get dragged into wars. I don’t even think his approach is that great either, as he’s usually a mindless brawler. He actually looked very sharp from a technical standpoint in his last fight, but Kopylov’s boxing is very ****** imo. When he actually started going first and put hands on Costa in r3, he was having a ton of success. He lost that fight because he just got owned with the body kick at range and had no answer for it. Costa’s body kick is nasty and it also presents itself in this Southpaw/Orthodox matchup, but I think there is a big deficiency in the boxing here. Azamat has this very unique Pankration style striking where he darts in/out very quickly and throws these lightning fast overhands. His striking is a little limited in terms of output but I find his process to be very high level; he has crazy fast hand speed and some of the best distance management in the division. Every fight he’s had in the UFC he’s been at a 5+ inch reach disadvantage (usually 7 inches or more) yet he always finds the target. Azamat is still the smaller guy funny enough but he’s facing someone closer to his size, and only has a half inch reach disparity here. I think this will not only make it easier for him to get his game off, but I think he is a far superior boxer to Costa. Azamat is going to land the overhands at will here since Costa has very poor defense, and I think he will have a ton of success if he gets Costa going backwards. Costa is a tank but he’s not invincible; he got rocked against Kopylov last fight and we’ve seen him stumbled/dropped on many other occasions. I think his defense is going to be a problem against someone as accurate as Azamat, and I’m also not sure how this lack of defense is going to translate against 205ers; especially a power puncher. To be fair, Azamat hasn’t slept anyone who hasn’t been ko’ed before, but he has multiple one punch ko’s and has dropped everyone he’s fought in the ufc. Costa is moving up a division and facing one of the hardest hitters he’s ever fought, which I don’t think will end well. I am a little shocked to see Tapology so heavy on KO as Costa is very durable, but I do think it’s a real possibility here. Either way, I think Azamat is not a great style for Costa. Yes he will slow down but Costa slows as well. He does fight through it better but he also gets very sloppy, meaning Azamat could sway the rounds back by landing a power shot; even when tired he has still been able to land big shots and sway the round/finish the fight. Costa is only a year younger than Azamat as well, so it’s not like he’s fighting a young up and comer. I think Costa is still kind of washed, he just looked good because he fought a massive step down in competition. I don’t think Azamat is elite but he’s clearly one of the more skilled LHWs and I do not think Costa is some great talent anymore.

Blaydes vs Hokit
Hokit KO RD 1

Another very tricky fight to call. I think Pickem/Blaydes slight fav makes a lot of sense here; this basically comes down to is Hokit at that level or not. I have been very adamant about the decline of Blaydes for a while now, and regardless of your opinion on the Kuniev fight, that fight clearly showed Blaydes is not an elite HW anymore. He does not use his wrestling much anymore and when he does it’s not even that effective. I am also of the opinion that HWs with a wrestling background can cause fits to Curtis; without his takedowns Blaydes is a slightly above average striker with a very bad chin. He’s still a very good striker by HW standards, but not one that should be a big favorite over anyone (hence why this fight is close to a pickem). There are still so many questions about Hokit so it’s hard for me to be confident; especially when I have been very critical of him in the past. I’m still not sure what to think of his ceiling but I was blown away by the Freeman fight. He may be a can but on paper he was not a great style for Hokit, yet Hokit treated him like a complete bum. Freeman can’t strike so it may not be a great litmus test, but the pace and striking improvements Hokit showed in that fight were very impressive; a big change from the guy who was running away from strikes on dwcs and limping after one leg kick from Gimenis just a few months ago. I don’t know if his cardio is any good and I tend to lean that it’s not, but he didn’t seem tired against Freeman, and that was a pace that 99% of HW would immediately fold under. I originally disliked the fact that Hokit was a LHW who doesn’t want to cut, but he has shown me that he’s clearly unaffected by the size discrepancy here; he’s just as strong as these guys. His athleticism alone already made him a better HW than most but he moves so fast considering he’s 20-40 lbs lighter than everyone in the division. Hokit has faced zero resistance to his wrestling whatsoever, and I have no clue what happens if he gets taken down either. However, I don’t see Blaydes having success getting him down; only if he gasses out. I think Blaydes wrestling has always been very overrated, especially against HWs who can actually wrestle. Blaydes is also at a big speed/athleticism discrepancy here, so even if he wants to wrestle I’m not sure he can actually get to the hips in time. I’m not relying on Hokit to have much success taking him down either, although it can be done. Blaydes can be taken down very easily, but holding him down is extremely difficult. Nobody has been able to score more than a couple seconds of control at a time. If Hokit tries to wrestle I see it being similar to the Jailton fight; he probably gets them with no resistance at all, but Blaydes will get up fairly easily. However, Hokit does a great job of firing off shots once he’s in close; he did this exact thing against Freeman, he got up after a few seconds so Hokit kept one hook on him and teed off with shots to make him uncomfortable. I’m not sure if it’s a great strategy in terms of energy management, but I do think he can make Blaydes absolutely miserable doing this. Cage pushing also takes away Blaydes huge reach advantage, which is probably his best weapon here. Blaydes has poor technical abilities and doesn’t even fight behind his reach that well, but he is a true HW and actually has striking, which is something Hokit has never had to deal with before. There is literally no footage of him absorbing a clean shot to the chin and he has faced next to no adversity, so I can’t speak on how he will deal with the striking. One thing is for sure though, if he blitzes Curtis early I think he will have a ton of success here. I tend to lean that Hokit will not react to getting hit well at all, but at the same time Blaydes is a very bad nail. I worry that Hokit will gas, but if he comes out how he did in his last fight and doesn’t allow Curtis to get started, I think he could actually dominate here. This is a very tricky fight to call and I feel like backing the Hokit side is more of a belief in his potential rather than what he’s shown. At the same time though, while a huge step up I don’t think Curtis is an elite HW at all anymore. He has a great skillset and the best resume at HW, but he’s also very chinny and slow these days. I feel like Hokit is catching him at the right time, Curtis has looked really poor against these HWs who can wrestle, and in a pure striking match I don’t think he’s really anything special. I will take the young, significantly faster HW with momentum here.

Reyes vs Walker
Reyes KO RD 1

This is amazing matchmaking, these two are very similar fighters both with similar career trajectory and glass jaws. This is an impossible fight to call as well, it quite literally comes down to who can find the chin first. Pre-tape I was liking Walker a good bit but after tape I’m really unsure of where to go. I have not liked picking Reyes nor did I believe in his resurgence; with a brutal KO like the last fight, I’m not sure he is going to have much left. Reyes’s run was cool but he had a lot of favorable matchmaking. I thought he looked horrendous against Ulberg prior to the KO as well; his head was not moving off the centerline (this is what caused him to drop 4 in a row) and he looked very timid the entire time. He reacted horribly to every shot landed and got slept the second he stepped into the pocket. This was making me side with Walker originally, but then I watched the Zhang fight and he looked godawful. Zhang had no idea how to cut off the cage and check leg kicks, but I still thought he was the better fighter there. Walker looked extremely slow which was really concerning given how he relies on being a freak athlete. He was very timid and low volume, as well as reacting poorly to every shot landed. Like I said, if Zhang knew how to cut off the cage I think he would’ve slept Walker inside of 2 minutes. He didn’t fight some masterful gameplan either, Zhang just got too plodding and didn’t know what a leg kick was; I like Zhang a lot but that was a fraudulent performance from him. Walker is also at XC now and I honestly think that gym is awful; Nicksick has turned everyone there into a low volume, counter strike heavy, point fighter and Walker seemed to take that same approach against Zhang. I also can’t forget how easily Volkan destroyed him, when all he did was pressure Walker. I could honestly see this going over as both may come out hesitant here. I think Walker knows he can’t take shots anymore and I didn’t like his passive approach in his last two. Reyes may come out doing the same since he just got brutally ko’ed yet again in his last outing; in this scenario, I slightly lean Reyes. I’ve always thought he was pretty overrated but I do believe he’s the more skilled of the two, Walker uses his athleticism as a crutch; I think his overall skillset is very meh, he’s just gigantic and freakishly athletic (although the latter appears to be declining a lot). I know it doesn’t mean much as he takes more damage, but wins over Jacoby and Krylov (especially how he beat them), are much more impressive than anything Walker has done in a long time (even if my opinion is that they were a little fluky). Walker did not look good against Zhang and his other wins this decade are a decision over Anthony Smith (where he again adopted this low volume sparring style), no chin Paul Craig, Ion Cutelaba, and Spann where he nearly ended up with the same fate as Reyes. I really hated Reyes' approach in the Ulberg fight, but Ulberg also has elite technical skills which Walker very much does not possess. This fight will come down to whoever lands the first shot but I don’t think Reyes is in danger of getting slept the way he did in his last fight; famous last words of course, but Walker hasn’t ko’ed someone with a precise 1-2 like that ever, he only wins via meme knockout. Which again, is very live here but in a fight that is going to come down to whoever finds the chin first, I think I like Reyes here. I have no confidence especially since I am usually on the side of fading both, but I think Reyes is the more skilled fighter here. Walker has also been extremely passive ever since the Craig fight, and in that case I think Reyes' skills/chin holds up better in that kind of fight. Walker has also looked lost any time he gets pressured recently, so I do think Reyes can be a little more aggressive than usual if he wants. Either way I expect someone to get viciously ko’ed here, although maybe it does get extended if both are respecting the other's power.

[Predictions]

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R1
Pitbull is pretty low volume and Pico should be able to handle his wrestling. Pico was throwing great body shots and younger pitbull would be able to handle it, but I think old Pitbull gets chinned here, especially since he got chinned not too long ago in Rizin. I don't think Pitbull has the power at this stage of his career to counter Pico with a big shot to put him out. Pico can probably swarm him and finish him. I am a little cautious to pick guys by KO after they've gotten KOed cause they tend to fight a bit more cautious, but Pico seems like the McKinney type fighter that doesn't care lol. For some reason, I have a much higher pick accuracy on higher level fighters coming over from other orgs to the UFC and picked Pitbull to beat Ige last fight, but I think he loses here

posts.content:
[Predictions]

Holland vs. Brown
Kevin Holland, SUB, R2
Randy Brown y Kevin Holland son prácticamente lo mismo, la diferencia esta en 2 años y 8 cms de alcance, esto a favor de Holland, en estadísticas estan prácticamente igual, una que otra cosa resalta para uno y otra para otro. Randy Brown aprovecha regularmente las salidas con cabeceo hacia atrás, cosa que creo que Holland y esa ventaja de alcance será una manera de dañar al Rudeboy. posts.author: TheAnalyzer
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R2
Damn it’s been a LONG time since I’ve last been on here! Hope everyone’s well. I just had leg surgery like 4 days ago, and dare I say this is a BANGER card to come home to!!!!! I think I’m going to settle with Jiri here as my prediction. I was initially going to place a bet on Ulberg, as he does have a bit of a Pereira-esque style which Jiri seems to struggle with a lot. Heavy low kicks, and timed power shots, somewhat patient striking and etc. But at the end of the day, Ulberg is simply not Pereira. He doesn’t hit nearly as hard, his leg kicks are also not nearly as effective, and he has a lot of flaws which I think can really do him in here. Ulberg can be rather open to getting hit a lot of the time, and historically he does not like being pressured. Jiri’s entire style is to pressure his opponent. Now obviously the Rountree fight was a bit strange as he didn’t do that until round 3, but when he finally did it, Rountree gassed like a **** and Jiri put him out cold. Ulberg can be a good counter striker at times against people who try to pressure him, but their pressure is nothing like Jiri. And Jiri also hits extremely hard. I’d argue he’s now the hardest hitter in the division now that Pereira’s going to 265 which is honestly mental since Jiri is the smallest one here. I also don’t see Ulberg having enough power to chin Jiri as he wasn’t able to finish people who don’t have amazing chins, and I don’t see him fighting perfectly for very long either once he realizes what he’s in for. He may be winning early, but eventually I think the pressure will get to him and Jiri will win by a late second round KO. I think this will be very similar to the Rakic fight for Jiri.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Azamat Murzakanov, DEC
I do not think highly of Murzakanov at all, but this is a rather easy fight for him. The main issues with Murzakanov are that he’s very short for this weight class, has close to no wingspan, and is very much a fluke merchant. That said, I actually think he wins this time without a fluke. I’m still not convinced that Costa takes his career seriously even after his last performance. Now I’ll give him credit where it’s due, it was a good performance. But that was against a very mid kickboxer who apparently forgot how to kickbox that night. Also despite being younger than Azamat in actual age (which is just insane), he’s definitely older in fight years. The injuries and infections throughout his career have definitely caught up to him over the past almost decade now. In fact, the last time Costa looked good before his last fight was all the way back in 2019 against a past-prime Yoel Romero. And you can argue he lost that fight too. Since then Izzy ****** him both doggy style and anally, he managed to get pieced up in moments by VETTORI, and couldn’t even chin Luke Rockhold who was WELL past his peak. And then he did have one good moment against Whittaker, but then got right back to getting pieced up. And then he looked scared ****less against Strickland (still baffles me how he was one scorecard away from winning due to that one crackhead judge (Dave Tirelli). So after all of that, I simply cannot trust Costa here. I am very much anti-Azamat but there’s nothing here that shows me he doesn’t win this fight. However I will say he wins by decision this time as Costa still actually has a great chin. And most of the people Azamat are finishing are not known for having the greatest chins ever. I can also see Costa potentially having a late rally even as he does have better cardio than Azamat as well. But I’m going to take the more serious, undefeated fighter who also just seems better at this point. Azamat 29-28.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, TKO, R1
If Hokit doesn’t get a flash KO he’s going to get pounded. My main worry for Blaydes is of course his chin, but also that he doesn’t wrestle as much as he used to. However I honestly do trust him to wrestle here. He actually wrestled the entire first round against Kuniev and pretty much dominated the round with it. Even Almeida(so happy he got cut) couldn’t do that. And while I do think Blaydes lost the rest of the fight with Kuniev, I also think Kuniev is quite solid. Maybe not elite, but good enough to hang with most of the division. Hokit on the other hand is honestly just a speed and power merchant. He swings and just manages to connect a kazillion power bombs on the chins of tomato cans. He does have a wrestling background himself and won his first few fights actually by submission, but I don’t think his wrestling is on the level of Blaydes. Blaydes also has Hokit cleared in almost every aspect of striking too except speed. If Hokit does connect one though he is probably going to sleep Blaydes. But if he can’t do that he has no other path to victory. Maybe a bet on Hokit by KO isn’t a bad idea, but there’s really only a 10% chance he wins this fight. I get he’s very marketable and the UFC want to push him, but unless Hokit can fluke it, he is not winning this fight.

Reyes vs. Walker
Dominick Reyes, TKO, R1
Flip a coin really. Both have the power of a hydrogen bomb, and the chin of a coughing baby. I’ll trust Reyes as he’s the more tactical striker in what will be a standup fight.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, TKO, R3
Winnable fight for Swanson but also entertaining as **** for his sendoff. I think Swanson chins the train late with a counter shot during a heated exchange. Happy Retirement Cub!

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R2
I still think Pico is very good, Pitbull is just washed at this point.

Holland vs. Brown
Kevin Holland, TKO, R2
Do not bet

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
Ribovics arguably lost to Zellhuber. Not picking him against anybody in the top 100.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Suarez seems to be on the decline but she should still win. Godinez’s defensive grappling isn’t great at all and I think Suarez will be able to control her. And maybe even get the guillotine.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, TKO, R2
I think this will just be a classic Chris Padilla fight. I don’t think either of these guys are necessarily good or bad, but I think Padilla’s finishing potential will make all of the difference. Plus he knows when he’s losing and will show urgency and push a finish when he feels like he needs to. I trust him more here.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, DEC
Both are washed but seeing Luque fight at this point of his career honestly makes me cry. He was at one point one of the most entertaining fighters on the roster. And then he got a brain bleed from Geoff Neal (who’s also washed now), and just doesn’t have it in him anymore.

Radtke vs. Prado
Charles Radtke, DEC
Radtke 30-27. Doesn’t find a finish this time. Prado the bum gets cut after.
